examples.title: Simulating Thrashing Detection
examples.description: Algorithm to detect if system is thrashing based on page fault rate and CPU utilization
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DetectThrashing
BEGIN
  SET pageFaultRate = 0
  SET cpuUtilization = 0
  SET THRESHOLD_HIGH = 0.7  // 70% time on page faults
  SET THRESHOLD_LOW = 0.2   // 20% CPU utilization
  
  REPEAT EVERY sampleInterval
    pageFaultRate = calculatePageFaultRate()
    cpuUtilization = calculateCpuUtilization()
    
    IF pageFaultRate > THRESHOLD_HIGH AND cpuUtilization < THRESHOLD_LOW THEN
      PRINT "WARNING: System is THRASHING"
      RETURN TRUE
    ELSE
      PRINT "System operating normally"
      RETURN FALSE
    ENDIF
  END REPEAT
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm monitors system metrics to detect thrashing. It calculates page fault rate and CPU utilization at regular intervals. If page fault rate is very high while CPU utilization is very low, it indicates thrashing.

examples.title: Page Fault Frequency Control Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm implementing PFF control for thrashing prevention
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PFFControl
BEGIN
  SET upperThreshold = 5   // faults per second
  SET lowerThreshold = 1   // faults per second
  SET minFrames = 3
  SET maxFrames = 50
  
  FOR EACH process P IN readyQueue
    faultRate = P.getPageFaultRate()
    
    IF faultRate > upperThreshold THEN
      // Process needs more frames
      IF P.framesAllocated < maxFrames THEN
        P.framesAllocated = P.framesAllocated + 1
      ENDIF
    ELSE IF faultRate < lowerThreshold THEN
      // Process has excess frames
      IF P.framesAllocated > minFrames THEN
        P.framesAllocated = P.framesAllocated - 1
      ENDIF
    ENDIF
  
  // If system is still thrashing, suspend a process
  IF systemThrashing() THEN
    suspendOneProcess()
  ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm implements PFF control by monitoring each process's fault rate. If the rate exceeds the upper threshold, frames are allocated. If below lower threshold, frames are reclaimed. If thrashing persists, a process is suspended.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of processes

examples.title: Working Set Calculation
examples.description: Python code to calculate working set for a process given reference string and window size τ
examples.code:
def calculate_working_set(reference_string, tau):
    """
    Calculate working set for given reference string and window size tau.
    Returns the set of unique pages in the working set window.
    """
    if tau <= 0 or len(reference_string) == 0:
        return set()
    
    # Get pages referenced in last tau references
    window_start = max(0, len(reference_string) - tau)
    window = reference_string[window_start:]
    
    working_set = set(window)
    return working_set

def simulate_thrashing(num_frames, reference_string, tau):
    """
    Simulate memory allocation and detect thrashing.
    Returns page fault count and whether thrashing occurs.
    """
    working_set_size = len(calculate_working_set(reference_string, tau))
    
    print(f"Available frames: {num_frames}")
    print(f"Working set size (τ={tau}): {working_set_size}")
    
    if working_set_size > num_frames:
        print("WARNING: Working set exceeds available frames!")
        print("Thrashing likely to occur.")
        return True
    else:
        print("Working set can fit in memory.")
        print("Thrashing unlikely.")
        return False

# Example usage
reference_string = [1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 2, 5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
num_frames = 3
tau = 4

thrash = simulate_thrashing(num_frames, reference_string, tau)
examples.explanation: This Python code calculates the working set of a process given a reference string and window size τ. It simulates whether thrashing would occur by comparing the working set size to available frames. If working set exceeds frames, thrashing is likely.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for reference string of length n
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m = number of unique pages

examples.title: LRU Page Replacement with Thrashing Detection
examples.description: C code demonstrating LRU page replacement algorithm with thrashing monitoring
examples.explanation: This C program implements the LRU (Least Recently Used) page replacement algorithm. It simulates page accesses and tracks page faults. If the page fault rate exceeds 50%, it warns about potential thrashing. LRU helps reduce thrashing by keeping recently used pages in memory.
